Safety Issues.finding: Exterior finish paint is failing and flaking in multiple areas, potentially exposing older lead-based paint and requiring professional remediation.
The step down from the rear deck to the yard is excessively high and presents a tripping hazard.
The door between the garage and the interior of the home is not fire-rated, reducing protection during a garage fire.
PEX water supply lines are installed within six inches of the water heater flue, creating a heat damage and fire risk.
An exterior receptacle tested ungrounded and did not trip when tested, presenting a shock and fire hazard.
Fireplace firebricks in the living room are loose, missing, or have deteriorated grout, compromising firebox safety.

Infestation.finding: Animal feces were observed in multiple attic locations, indicating current or prior animal intrusion requiring removal and sanitation.
Firewood is stored too close to the structure, which can attract termites and other wood-destroying insects.

Evaluate.finding: Temporary support posts were observed in the crawlspace and should be evaluated and replaced with permanent supports.
Rafter spread was observed along the attic ridge in several locations and requires further structural evaluation.
Interior floors slope in multiple areas of the home and should be evaluated by a qualified contractor.
No evidence of recent HVAC servicing was observed, and a qualified HVAC contractor should inspect and service the system.

Minor Concerns.finding: Drip edge flashing is missing in areas of the roof.
At least one plumbing vent does not meet required clearance from the roof surface.
Gutters contain debris that should be cleaned to prevent concealed rust or leaks.
Brick veneer is in contact with soil or landscaping in areas, which can trap moisture.
Brick veneer drainage holes are missing or blocked in several locations.
Light cracking is present in exterior brick veneer.
The garage ceiling does not provide proper air separation from the attic.
The garage floor has several small cracks.
Daylight is visible around the rear entry garage door when closed.
Flooring shows delamination and separation in multiple areas.
Interior doors are missing door stoppers or stoppers do not function properly.
One set of bedroom closet doors does not close completely.
A small gap is present between a window and the sill at the laundry room.
A toilet at the owner's bath is loose at the floor and may require resetting with a new wax seal.
At least two waste lines in the crawlspace appear to have improper slope.
The water heater flue is missing required fasteners at multiple joints.
Several three-prong electrical outlets are loose in walls throughout the home.
The fireplace damper lid is missing or not installed.
The exterior air conditioning condenser is dirty and requires cleaning.
Attic insulation levels are light in most areas.
The crawlspace vapor barrier is missing in areas.
Bathroom exhaust ducts terminate in the attic rather than venting to the exterior.

Prepare.finding: Exterior light fixtures should be sealed to the wall surface to prevent moisture intrusion.
Cracked or deteriorated grout in the hall bathroom tile walls should be repaired.
A loose handle on the fireplace door should be repaired or replaced.
Dryer vents longer than ten feet or installed vertically should be cleaned annually to reduce fire risk.
